name: Task Management
copyPolicy: Use English labels as working copy/placeholders unless Arabic copy is provided. Preserve space for Arabic translation.
productPurpose: Dense work queue for finding, reviewing, editing, and actioning S-System tasks.
commonUserWork: Review an incoming task from the list.
Filter or search the queue to find the right task.
Open a selected task and review its preview/detail content.
Create a new task or continue editing a task draft.
Update task sections such as linked records, documents, attachments, comments, or extra fields.
Use visible action buttons to move the task forward when the screen state calls for it.

canonicalReference.editPolicy: Treat as canonical reference. Create an instance, duplicate, or create a new variant/mockup unless the user explicitly asks to edit this component.

topBar.title: Tasks
topBar.placementRule: Use for global task scope, task creation, list view mode, sorting, and top-level task tabs.

filtersPanel.heading: Filters
filtersPanel.newFilterDefaultPlacement: Inside AppFilters content. For task metadata filters, place near related metadata fields and keep existing row spacing.
filtersPanel.newFilterPriorityExamplePlacement: After Work status and before Color unless product guidance says otherwise.

taskList.selectedState: Use selected TaskItem state when the preview/detail area reflects that task.
taskList.businessSignalRule: Rows should include enough signal for scanning: task ID, subject, type/subtype icon, status, date, assignee/from avatars, attachments, urgency/freeze indicators when relevant, and selected state when tied to the preview.

modes.label: Colorful
modes.rule: Use configured icon palette colors from components/icon/task_types/*.

modes.label: Monochrome
modes.rule: Use neutral monochrome styling while preserving icon size, shape, and placement.

taskTypeSubtypeIconAppearance.drawingRule: Do not represent icon appearance as a Task Management filter. Draw the preference control in User Preferences / Appearance and show Task Management as the result example.

previewDetail.changeRule: Do not change this area for filter-only work except to keep selected-task context coherent.
previewDetail.emptyStateRule: If no task is selected, show a compact empty or instructional state rather than leaving the panel blank.

uxStates.rule: One task row is selected and the preview/detail island reflects that task.
Keep filters visible and show a clear empty state in the list area.
Keep the shell stable and show loading inside the list or preview region.
Show editable state and save/continue actions.
Make the primary next action visible near the task detail or action bar.
Show the issue next to the task section or action that needs correction.

drawingRules: Read this JSON and app/apps/task-management.md before drawing Task Management features.
Inspect the canonical component before changing layouts.
Use AppFilters for persistent filters by default.
Use published SUI components from the local component index by componentKey.
Keep RTL alignment and preserve Arabic expansion space.
Keep 8px island gaps unless explicitly redesigning the layout.
Use background/surface_2 for list/filter islands and background/surface_3 for cards and input surfaces.
Keep task actions close to the selected task context; only use global navigation for global actions.
Create an instance, duplicate, or create a new mockup frame rather than modifying the canonical reference by default.

relatedFlows.description: System-wide Colorful/Monochrome icon appearance preference configured in User Preferences, with TaskItem type/subtype icons as the primary result example.

openQuestions: Which filters belong behind More versus visible by default?
Where active filters should be represented when applied.
Exact empty, loading, error, and no-results states.
Saved search creation and management behavior.
Whether Priority and Urgent are separate concepts.
